behavior_summary: When an actor enters the door's fast-area trigger (U8P_LOCKDOOR::enterFastArea), the script decides whether the door item should be locked or unlocked by setting/clearing a lock bit in the item's quality word and immediately applies that change.

Decision logic is driven by an egg id (ownership/region/actor identifier) returned by Egg::getEggId(this), per-egg branching that consults several global schedule/plot flags and a random roll, and by current item-quality flags.
If the item has the auto-use bit (0x0100) set the code spawns DOOR_NS::useDoor(this) instead of only toggling flags, storing the spawn result and suspending the process.
A special case for egg id 0x0A: when global flags acolyteKilled > 0 and goToInnerSanctum == 1 the door is forced unlocked and a runtime status flag (0x0080) is toggled on the object.
notable_control_flow: Read egg id (Egg::getEggId) then branch on its value to apply per-egg rules for locking/unlocking.
For at least one egg id, a random roll urandom(0x64) is compared (against 0x32) to produce probabilistic locking behavior.
Item::getQuality reads the item's flag word into a local; code sets/clears the lock bit (inferred 0x0400) and calls Item::setQuality to write it back.
If auto-use bit (inferred 0x0100) is present the routine spawns DOOR_NS::useDoor and suspends rather than only changing flags.
Special-casing reads of rhianSchedule, araminaSchedule, korickSchedule, acolyteKilled and goToInnerSanctum influence lock decisions for specific egg ids.
open_questions: Precise semantic meaning of each egg id (local2): owner, region, NPC, or other grouping? (inferred, not explicit).
Exact semantics of quality bits: 0x0400, 0x0100 and 0x0080 are inferred from usage (lock bit, auto-use, runtime status toggle) but not named in the script.
Specific numeric schedule values that indicate 'open' vs 'closed' for rhianSchedule/araminaSchedule/korickSchedule are inferred from comparisons and not explicit.
Whether spawning DOOR_NS::useDoor always implies immediate visual open animation or can fail; spawn result/pid usage is observed but its downstream handling is not shown.

main_function.name: U8P_LOCKDOOR::enterFastArea
main_function.purpose: Handle a fast-area entry for a door object: determine and apply lock/unlock state (and optionally auto-use the door) based on egg id, schedules/plot flags, random chance, and item-quality bits.
entry_conditions: U8P_LOCKDOOR::enterFastArea trigger (actor/avatar enters the door's trigger region)
this (the door item) exists and can be queried/modified

function_map.name: U8P_LOCKDOOR::enterFastArea
function_map.role: door lock/unlock handler invoked on fast-area entry (may spawn door use when auto-use bit set)

external_calls.purpose: Obtain an egg id for this object to select per-id locking logic (ownership/region/NPC identifier).
Produce a 0..99 random roll (urandom(0x64)) used for probabilistic locking decisions.
Read the door item's quality/flag word into a local so lock/auto-use bits can be inspected.
Write the modified quality/flag word back to the door item after setting/clearing lock and other bits.
Spawn a door-use (open/operate) process when the item's auto-use bit is set instead of just toggling flags.
Toggle a runtime status flag (inferred 0x0080) on the door object for a special progression case (acolyteKilled + goToInnerSanctum).

reads.name: rhianSchedule
reads.likely_effect: Influences lock state for egg id 0x02 (used to decide clear/set of lock bit).

reads.name: araminaSchedule
reads.likely_effect: Influences lock state for egg id 0x03 (used to decide clear/set of lock bit).

reads.name: korickSchedule
reads.likely_effect: For egg id 0x05, certain values (0x01 or 0x03 inferred) cause lock bit to be cleared (unlock).

reads.name: acolyteKilled
reads.likely_effect: When >0 and combined with goToInnerSanctum forces door unlock for egg id 0x0A and triggers a status-flag toggle.

reads.name: goToInnerSanctum
reads.likely_effect: Combined with acolyteKilled to control unlocking and toggling of runtime status for egg id 0x0A.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: When an actor enters the door's fast-area trigger (U8P_LOCKDOOR::enterFastArea), the script decides whether the door item should be locked or unlocked by setting/clearing a lock bit in the item's quality word and immediately applies that change.
